channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Veterans Struggling to Access Healthcare Amid Defence Technology Spending
item.description: The article highlights concerns over the Australian government’s failure to adequately support veterans, despite promises made on Anzac Day. The Department of Veterans' Affairs (DVA) has faced critici...

item.title: Why diagnosing public figures with dementia from afar is unreliable and ethically problematic
item.description: Recent public debate has centred on speculation about US President Donald Trump’s mental fitness, with some commentators suggesting conditions such as frontotemporal dementia to explain his behaviour....

item.title: Nigerian Guild of Editors raises concern over NBC’s sanction threats to broadcasters
item.description: The Nigerian Guild of Editors (NGE) don express worry say National Broadcasting Commission (NBC) fit start sanction broadcast presenters wey dem think dey mix personal opinion with facts or dey bully ...

item.title: Authorities urged to prioritise crowd control and safety at Soweto derby
item.description: The article stresses the urgent need for improved safety and crowd management at the upcoming Soweto derby between Orlando Pirates and Kaizer Chiefs, following serious failures during the previous mat...

item.title: Lesufi disputes allegations of requesting sensitive police dockets amid Madlanga Commission inquiry
item.description: Gauteng Premier Panyaza Lesufi has rejected allegations that he instructed police officials to hand over sensitive case dockets, including those linked to J50 arrest warrants, describing the claims as...

item.title: US-Kuwaiti Journalist Ahmed Shihab-Eldin Acquitted and Released After 52 Days in Kuwait Detention
item.description: Ahmed Shihab-Eldin, a prominent US-Kuwaiti journalist known for contributions to The New York Times, PBS, Al Jazeera English, HBO, Vice, BBC, and others, was detained in Kuwait on March 3, 2026, durin...

item.title: BMW Integrates E Ink into iX3 Flow Edition Hood for Color Changes
item.description: BMW has advanced its color-changing vehicle technology with the iX3 Flow Edition concept, unveiled at the 2026 Beijing Auto Show. Unlike previous concepts that wrapped the entire car in custom-sized E...

item.title: Archaeological Study Confirms Mass Grave from Plague of Justinian in Ancient Jordan
item.description: A newly confirmed mass grave in Jerash, Jordan, provides rare insight into the Plague of Justinian, one of the earliest recorded pandemics, which occurred between 541 and 750 CE. Researchers from the ...

item.title: Ouaddou calls for focused and humble approach ahead of Soweto derby
item.description: Orlando Pirates coach Abdeslam Ouaddou has called on his players to remain grounded and approach the upcoming Soweto derby against Kaizer Chiefs with humility, despite their recent dominance in the fi...

item.title: US Justice Department proposes additional execution methods including firing squads
item.description: WASHINGTON: The US Justice Department has announced plans to broaden the scope of the federal death penalty and introduce new execution methods alongside lethal injection. Acting Attorney General Todd...

item.title: Surge in cyber breaches threatens millions of South Africans
item.description: A recent rise in cybersecurity breaches has left millions of South Africans vulnerable, with key state and private institutions falling victim to attacks. These breaches have compromised sensitive med...

item.title: Final Year of Miss Indian World Pageant Concludes a Longstanding Cultural Tradition
item.description: The Miss Indian World pageant, a key feature of the Gathering of Nations powwow—the largest in North America—will hold its final event in 2025. Since its inception in 1984, the pageant has provided In...

item.title: Avi Lewis Prepares to Eulogize His Father Stephen Lewis in a Memorial Service
item.description: Avi Lewis, the recently elected federal leader of the NDP, is preparing for a highly personal eulogy at a memorial service for his late father, Stephen Lewis. Stephen, who passed away just days after ...

item.title: Journalist Ahmed Shihab-Eldin Released from Kuwaiti Detention and Departs the Country
item.description: Ahmed Shihab-Eldin, a U.S.-Kuwaiti journalist, was released from detention in Kuwait after being arrested on March 3, 2026. His arrest followed a crackdown on individuals posting videos related to the...

item.title: Prosecution requests ICC to make public Duterte defense response involving six lawyers
item.description: The International Criminal Court (ICC) is being urged by the Prosecution to reclassify a defense response submitted by former Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte's legal team. The issue arises becaus...

item.title: Dangote Urges Nigeria to Invest in Local Manufacturing and Power Infrastructure
item.description: Aliko Dangote, the oga at the Dangote Group, don yan say make Nigeria dey focus on local manufacturing and power infrastructure sharply. During the Nigerian Academy of Engineering induction ceremony f...

item.title: Hazariibag Resident Dies in Lake Fishing Accident
item.description: A tragic incident unfolded in Hazariibag, India, where a man lost his life while fishing in a local lake. Details surrounding the incident are still emerging, but authorities are investigating the cir...

item.title: Government increases petrol and diesel prices to meet IMF fiscal targets
item.description: ISLAMABAD: The federal government led by Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif on Friday implemented an unexpected increase in petrol and diesel prices by Rs26.77 per litre, effective immediately until May 1....

item.title: Philippine official confirms arrest and detention of Zaldy Co in Prague
item.description: Kinumpirma ni Pangulong Ferdinand Marcos Jr. na si Zaldy Co ay naaresto at kasalukuyang nakadetine sa Prague, Czech Republic. Ayon sa ulat, ang kumpirmasyon ay nagmula mismo sa Pangulo, bagaman limita...

item.title: Samsung Faces Potential First-Ever Annual Loss in Mobile Division
item.description: Samsung’s mobile division could face its first-ever annual loss in 2026, according to a recent report. Factors contributing to this financial challenge include rising memory costs, increasing competit...
